title: Magnet Power Supplies
claim_supported: OCEM offers magnet power supplies up to 50,000A with stability/resolution up to 5ppm, serving CERN, Diamond Light Source, hadron-therapy centers, and fusion (JT-60SA, DTT); >3,500 converters delivered over 35+ years.
locator: Product page body, specifications and market-reach sections; fetched 2026-07-13
notes: P4-required incumbency-map source for P3R2-F-02: confirms precision magnet converters are a mature catalog category, narrowing but not eliminating the idea's claimed white space (integrated extraction/dump + leads + acceptance-protocol island).

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: OCEM Power Electronics is headquartered in Valsamoggia, Bologna, Italy (confirmed via company LinkedIn page). Not India.

title: Products — Power Supplies, Magnets, Electrostatic Devices
claim_supported: Danfysik manufactures high-stability power supplies 'from a few watts to megawatts and stability from 0.1ppm' and custom magnets up to 100+ metric tons, serving accelerators and research facilities globally.
notes: Second P4-required incumbency-map source for P3R2-F-02; tighter stability spec (0.1ppm) than F-02's own 10ppm-class target, confirming precision converters are a mature, competed category.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Danfysik A/S is a long-established Danish precision power-supply/magnet manufacturer (Jyllinge, Denmark). Not India.

title: Realta Fusion and Commonwealth Fusion Systems Form Strategic Partnership to Commercialize Magnetic Mirror Fusion Energy
claim_supported: Published 2026-04-02. CFS and Realta Fusion signed a long-term strategic HTS-magnet partnership with 'the potential to reach a multi-billion dollar value'; CFS will build magnets for Realta's Anvil and commercial-scale Hammir devices and first-of-a-kind power plants targeted for 'the mid-2030s.'
notes: Primary named-buyer demand evidence for P3R2-F-02's US leg (confirms the merchant-magnet market F-02's BOP platform would serve is real); also a primary 2030s timing trigger.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Both companies are US-based (CFS: Devens, MA; Realta Fusion: Madison, WI). PRNewswire (Cision) is a US wire distributor. Not India.

claim_supported: DOE's Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program is 'authorized for a total of $415 million through fiscal year 2027' under the CHIPS and Science Act, with $46M obligated for the first 18-month period across 8 selectees; the most aggressive awardees target 'FPP preliminary-design reviews by the late 2020s' toward 'an operating FPP by the mid-2030s.'
locator: Article body, program-funding and awardee-progress sections; fetched 2026-07-13
notes: Independent second US demand source (federal program funding, separate from the CFS-Realta commercial deal) and primary official 2028-2035 timing trigger for P3R2-F-02.

title: 聚变堆超导磁体研发取得新突破 (Fusion-reactor superconducting magnet R&D achieves new breakthrough)
authors_or_org: Hefei Institutes of Physical Science, Chinese Academy of Sciences
claim_supported: Published 2026-06-27. ASIPP/Hefei Institutes completed and parameter-tested a 582-tonne toroidal-field magnet (14.5T, 120GJ stored energy, 98kA, '1.3x the volume of ITER's TF magnet, 3x the stored energy') and a high-Tc central-solenoid coil (60kA, 6.03MJ), both stated '100%国产化' (100% domestically produced); no power-supply/converter procurement is mentioned.
notes: Confirms ASIPP is actively building large-scale, domestic-content superconducting magnet systems that structurally require drive/dump/lead/busbar hardware, but provides no dated tender or procurement record for that hardware class -- the idea's named CN buyer claim (ASIPP PF-converter/RMP tenders) remains unconfirmed as buyer evidence.

title: 昆泰磁悬浮：让泵"浮"起来实现反超 (Kuntai Maglev: making the pump 'float' to achieve a leapfrog)
claim_supported: Published 2025-06-05. Kuntai (昆泰磁悬浮) claims to be 'the only domestic enterprise that has broken the foreign technology monopoly on semiconductor-grade magnetic levitation molecular pumps'; its KTP-304 model ('pumping speed 350-570L/s, exceeding mainstream international products by over 20%') is priced 'only 70%-80% of imported equipment,' with orders scheduled through H2 2025.
notes: Direct evidence that CN domestic AMB/maglev-turbopump substitution is already commercially active and price-aggressive, confirming there is no design-in channel for a merchant (especially non-Chinese) AMB electronics supplier in this exact niche -- key finding supporting the CN-leg downgrade for P3R2-F-04.

notes: Confirms State Grid UHV secondary-equipment procurement is real, large, dated, and won by domestic incumbents (Xuji/NR ecosystem) -- directly supports treating P3R2-F-06's CN chapter as license-royalty-only (domestic-only procurement structure), not a counted beachhead, per the idea's binding downgrade.

india_origin_audit.notes: Yicai (China Business News / Yicai Media Group) is a Chinese financial-media company headquartered in Shanghai, China. Not India.
